page: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor using the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 caches, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including VNNI and BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

This server configuration does not include local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. It does not feature any onboard GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple domains. Both single-core and multi-core CPU stress tests place the instance in the top 10% of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes reaching top-tier status, while large-block uncached reads fall into the top 25%. Computational efficiency is further highlighted by top-tier results in gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and PassMark CPU tests. Database and memory operations are also highly performant, with Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks scoring in the top 10%.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-density compute capabilities, the D96as_v7 offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive enterprise workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is highly suited for large-scale web serving, database hosting, multi-threaded data processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference, as evidenced by its top-tier performance in LLM prompt processing and text generation benchmarks.
description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores and is paired with 384.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but is Premium Storage capable. In benchmark testing, the D96as_v7 delivers top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference. This configuration is highly suited for demanding enterprise workloads, including database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and memory performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and top-tier CPU and database benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-core-density general-purpose virtual machine delivering top-tier compute and memory performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of system memory
Top-tier single-core and multi-core CPU benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Excellent memory bandwidth and database operation throughput
Well-suited for enterprise databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
